message:
fix(runtime): simplify hardware_planner capacity exhaustion to direct rejection instead of fake fallback

The hardware_planner's placement rejection path previously emitted a
misleading three-event sequence (HWP-007 → HWP-008 → HWP-010) that
simulated a "fallback attempt" which always failed because it re-scanned
the same already-filtered profile set. This created confusing audit logs
suggesting a secondary pool was consulted when none existed, and the
PlacementOutcome::RejectedFallbackExhausted / ERR_HWP_FALLBACK_EXHAUSTED
error code obscured the actual root cause (capacity exhaustion).

Changes:
- Remove the fake fallback path (HWP-008, HWP-010 events) and the
  fallback_attempted/fallback_reason evidence fields
- Emit only HWP-007 ("all capable+risk-ok targets at capacity") when
  no targets have remaining slots
- Return PlacementOutcome::RejectedCapacityExhausted /
  HardwarePlannerError::CapacityExhausted with a direct, honest error
  code (ERR_HWP_CAPACITY_EXHAUSTED)
- Update doc comment to reflect actual event range (HWP-004..HWP-007)
- Update tests: rename placement_rejected_capacity_exhausted_fallback →
  placement_rejected_capacity_exhausted, assert new error code and
  outcome variant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
